data.title: New York regulator approves 29 infrastructure projects valued at $636M
data.description:
The New York State Public Service Commission said Thursday it approved 29 infrastructure projects worth $636.2M, enabling 642 MW of electrification upgrades.

The projects were proposed by Consolidated Edison (NYSE:ED), National Grid's (NYSE:NGG) Niagara Mohawk Power Corporation, New York State Electric & Gas Corporation, and Rochester Gas and Electric Corporation to address existing and near-term infrastructure in support of transportation and building electrification.

Con Edison (NYSE:ED) will lead five projects, including a significant expansion at Zerega Avenue to enable fleet electrification and a redesign of the Hunt’s Point electrical system, adding 380 MW of capacity at a cost of nearly $440M.

National Grid (NYSE:NGG) will oversee 22 projects totaling $126B for 184.8 MW of new capacity in the form of mobile energy storage devices for near-term high-power charging in upstate New York, while NYSEG will implement a $37M industrial electrification project adding 30 MW, and a $33M project by RG&E will expand EV charging infrastructure by 47 MW.

"Taken together, these projects represent a major step in modernizing and expanding New York’s electrical system and will accelerate the state’s progress towards its decarbonization goals," the PSC said.

data.title: Kenvue considering selling some skin, beauty brands - report
data.description:
• Kenvue is weighing the sale of some its skin health and beauty brands.
  • Clean & Clear, Maui Moisture, and Neostrata are among the brands potentially being looked at, Reuters reported, citing sources. Bebe, a German baby care brand, and Japanese brand Dr.Ci:Labo could also be included.
  • Neutrogena and Aveeno, two of the larger brands in Kenvue's portfolio, are said to not be under consideration.
  • Sources told Reuters that the brands that could be sold comprise ~$500M in revenue.
  • Goldman Sachs is reportedly advising Kenvue on the potential divestment.

data.title: NextDecade finalizes $9B in contracts with Bechtel for Rio Grande LNG construction work
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) +3.7% post-market Thursday after saying it has finalized contracts with Bechtel to construct train 4 and train 5 at its Rio Grande LNG facility, with a combined value of ~$9B.

Under engineering, procurement, and construction contracts, NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) said it agreed to pay Bechtel ~$4.77B for work on Rio Grande LNG Train 4 and ~$4.32B for work on Rio Grande LNG Train 5.

NextDecade (NEXT) said it expects the pricing validity under the contracts to extend through September 15, and it aims to achieve a positive final investment decision on train 4 before the end of the pricing validity period for its EPC contract.

The company recently announced a 20-year, 2M metric tons/year LNG sale and purchase agreement with JERA for offtake from Train 5 and is seeking to commercialize an additional 2.5M tons/year under long-term LNG SPAs to support a positive FID on Train 5.

data.title: Elanco gains positive EMA opinion for Zenrelia for itching in dogs
data.description:
• The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Veterinary Medicinal Products has issued a positive opinion for Elanco Animal Health's (NYSE:ELAN) Zenrelia (ilunocitinib) for the treatment of pruritus (itching) associated with allergic dermatitis in dogs.
  • Elanco plans a launch after receiving formal marketing authorization with an eye toward Q3.
  • Zenrelia is already approved in the U.S., Canada, Japan, and Brazil. Approvals in Australia and the U.K. are expected later this year.

data.title: Neuralink files to raise $649M in financing
data.description:
• Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) founder Elon Musk's neurotechnology company, Neuralink, has filed for a $649M equity offering. 
  • An SEC filing dated Thursday indicates that the offering will not last more than a year. 
  • Neuralink designs brain-computer interfaces that can be surgically implanted. These implants allow the user to control electronic devices, such as smartphones or computers, with their thoughts alone. The technology is intended to help people with paralysis or other debilitating neurological conditions the ability to gain more autonomy and independence.
  • Musk also runs Starlink (STRLK), SpaceX (SPACE), xAI and X.

data.title: Gold shoots higher on simmering geopolitical tensions, weaker U.S. dollar
data.description:
Gold futures climbed towards the $3,400/oz level on Thursday, as geopolitical and trade tensions prompted a selloff in the U.S. dollar, which helped turn investors back towards favoring precious metals as a safe haven.

President Trump said the U.S. was moving personnel out of the Middle East because it "could be a dangerous place," and Iran's defense minister said on Wednesday that his country would retaliate if attacked by striking U.S. bases in the region.

"The longer the Iran situation continues to smolder, the greater safe haven flows into the shiny metal," Mizuho's Robert Yawger said in a note, adding that he anticipates gold will retest its record highs as long as the turmoil continues.

Trade-related headlines added to the mix, as Trump said that he was willing to extend the July 8 deadline for completing trade talks, but also that he is ready to impose unilateral tariffs within two weeks to multiple trading counterparts.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery (XAUUSD:CUR) finished +1.8% to $3,380.90/oz, its best settlement value since May 7 and fifth-highest close in history, while front-month Comex silver for June delivery (XAGUSD:CUR) edged up by +0.1% to $36.213/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (SPLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: XTI Aerospace launches public offering of up to 3.96M shares, pre-funded warrants
data.description:
• XTI Aerospace (NASDAQ:XTIA) is conducting a firm commitment public offering of up to 3.96M shares of common stock.
  • Shares are priced at $5.05 each.
  • Purchasers exceeding 4.99% (or 9.99%, by election) ownership may instead receive pre-funded warrants.
  • Up to 3.96M pre-funded warrants will be offered.
  • Pre-funded warrants are immediately exercisable and remain so until fully exercised.
  • The offering includes the underlying shares issuable upon exercise of the warrants.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Air India crash is likely to draw scrutiny to airplane configuration at take-off
data.description:
A catastrophic crash involving an Air India Boeing (NYSE:BA) 787-8 just seconds after takeoff from Ahmedabad is expected to center investigators’ attention on the aircraft’s configuration during the critical early phase of flight, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing aviation safety experts.

The London-bound jet went down in a residential area less than a minute after departure, killing 241 of the 242 people on board. It marks the first fatal crash involving Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) Dreamliner aircraft.

Indian authorities have opened an inquiry with support from the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board, which will send investigators to assist in the probe. The investigation is expected to rely heavily on the aircraft’s flight-data recorder, cockpit-voice recorder and video footage captured by bystanders in the flight’s final moments.

Flight-tracking data from Flightradar24 indicates the aircraft reached an altitude of 625 feet in clear conditions before it abruptly stopped transmitting location data, just 50 seconds after takeoff.

According to aviation experts cited by the Journal, several early indicators suggest investigators will likely focus on whether the aircraft was correctly configured for takeoff.

Key among those factors are the position of the flaps and slats, the movable surfaces on the wings that increase lift during takeoff. Initial analysis has raised questions about whether those systems were extended as required, John Cox, an aviation-safety consultant and former airline pilot, said to the Journal.

The Dreamliner is equipped with a warning system that alerts pilots if the aircraft is not properly configured for takeoff. Investigators are likely to seek to determine whether that system functioned correctly, and if so, whether the crew received and responded to the alert.

The aircraft’s landing gear also appeared to still be extended during flight, a detail that could be relevant to drag and lift performance calculations. Experts noted that while a delay in retracting the gear is not unusual, its combination with a possible lift deficit and increasing pitch could have been a dangerous mix.

Engine performance will also be closely examined. According to Jeff Guzzetti, a former senior investigator with both the FAA and NTSB, the absence of yaw or rudder deflection in the aircraft’s final trajectory may indicate that a single-engine failure is unlikely, the Journal reported. 

The Dreamliner involved in the crash was 11 years old. While the 787 is among Boeing’s (BA) bestselling widebody aircraft, the program has faced several production disruptions in the past decade, including quality-control issues that temporarily paused deliveries.

Air India, formerly state-run, was acquired by Tata Sons in 2022. The airline has since placed a record aircraft order in a bid to modernize its fleet and improve its safety record. The last fatal incident involving the carrier occurred in 2020, when an Air India Express plane overran a runway in Kerala.

data.title: Onto Innovation appoints Brian Roberts as CFO
data.description:
• Onto Innovation (NYSE:ONTO) has appointed Brian Roberts as chief financial officer and Shirley Chen as senior vice president of customer success, effective as of June 16, 2025, the company said on Thursday.
  • Mark Slicer will be transitioning out of Onto Innovation and pursuing other opportunities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Quince Therapeutics announces pricing of up to $22M private placement; shares up 22%
data.description:
• Quince Therapeutics (NASDAQ:QNCX) announces a private placement expected to generate $11.5M in upfront proceeds.
  • The deal includes common stock (or pre-funded warrants) and accompanying common warrants.
  • Warrants could bring in an additional $10.4 M if exercised in full for cash.
  • Funds will support working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • QNCX shares rise 22% post-market on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release
